species: Coculture: Escherichia coli PFM2 + Rhodopseudomonas palustris CGA676 (NifA*)

growth_records.strain: E. coli PFM2 (MG1655 rpoS+ rph+) + R. palustris CGA676 NifA*

up.method: 25 mM glucose consumed by E. coli; qualitative

sec.method: E. coli fermentation products consumed by R. palustris (no acidification, unlike monoculture)
R. palustris N2 fixation product, consumed by E. coli
produced by both partners; additional adenine and iron cross-feeding inferred

growth_records.cite: Chuang, Ying-Chih et al. Reciprocal cross-feeding between bacteria can limit the emergence of metabolic dependencies. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.1128/aem.00363-26. [PMC13274399]
growth_records.snippet: Cocultures were fed 25 mM glucose. Growth of each species is dependent on the exchange of NH4+... E. coli ferments glucose into organic acids that provide essential carbon to R. palustris... R. palustris fixes N2, producing NH4+ and H2.
growth_records.notes: Obligate cross-feeding anaerobic coculture (carbon/nitrogen exchange) on defined MDC with 25 mM glucose (only stated component). Higher glucose (25 mM) tolerated vs 10 mM monoculture because R. palustris consumes acidifying organic acids. Additional adenine and iron cross-feeding inferred. All exchange fluxes qualitative; numeric growth rates in figures only, left null. Experimental-evolution study.
